name: ground_truth_ac3_access_enforcement_compliant
description: Ground-truth fixture from active Example System IATT CCIS workbook (row 31, CCI-000213, assessed 2026-05-27). Compliant verdict based on RBAC implementation review on Linux hosts and container orchestration. Narrative uses 'Reviewed' and 'confirmed' affirming phrases. Evidence type: technical configuration (STIG-related RBAC, sudoers, container RBAC).

ccis_row.guidance: 000213: The information system enforces approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
ccis_row.procedures: The organization conducting the inspection/assessment examines the information system to ensure the organization being inspected/assessed configures the information system to enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- RBAC configuration export (RHEL 8 hosts) — sudoers configuration and group membership assignments for logical access enforcement.
- Container orchestration RBAC policy — role bindings and cluster-role assignments enforcing authorized access at the platform layer.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined RBAC implementation on RHEL 8 hosts and container orchestration platform; confirmed via group membership, sudoers, and platform-level RBAC that approved authorizations are enforced for logical access.
